commit.message:
fix: add @Inject() to all controllers/services for tsx compatibility

Extends the previous EventsModule fix to all controllers and services.
Without explicit @Inject() decorators, tsx/esbuild cannot resolve
constructor parameters by type, causing undefined service injections
and 500 errors on all API endpoints.

Also fixes the Vite dev proxy to rewrite /api prefix before forwarding
to the control plane, which serves routes without the /api prefix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add explicit @Inject() to EventsModule services for tsx compatibility

tsx (esbuild) does not support emitDecoratorMetadata, so NestJS cannot
resolve constructor parameters by type alone. Services using explicit
@Inject(TOKEN) worked, but EventsModule services relying on implicit
type-based resolution for ModuleRef received undefined, crashing
QueueWorkerEventsService.onModuleInit on startup.

Add @Inject() to all constructor parameters in:
- QueueWorkerEventsService (EventBroadcasterService, ModuleRef)
- DomainEventBroadcasterAdapter (EventBroadcasterService, ModuleRef)
- EventBroadcasterService (EventsGateway)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(hub): add OIDC authentication scaffolding (#100)

- Add auth module with OidcConfig, AuthUser, JWT claim decoding,
  auth middleware, and auth routes (login, callback, logout, me)
- When OIDC_ENABLED is unset (default), all requests pass through
  as anonymous; when enabled, Bearer JWT is required on API routes
- Wire auth middleware on /api/v1/* routes via Router::nest
- Add /auth/login, /auth/callback (501 placeholders), /auth/logout,
  /auth/me endpoints outside the auth-protected API nest
- Add actor field to AuditEntry for user attribution in audit logs
- Update connect/disconnect handlers to log authenticated user email
- Add base64 dependency for JWT payload decoding

Full Azure Entra ID integration (discovery, token validation, PKCE)
requires client registration and is out of scope for this scaffold.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(web): wire API layer to hub HTTP endpoints (#98)

Replace webFallback() stub returns with real fetch calls to the hub
service at /api/v1/*. All read operations (contexts, pods, resources,
events, logs, helm, metrics, CRDs, search, etc.) now hit the hub.
Write operations (scale, delete, apply, exec, port-forward, helm
rollback) are deferred to a future iteration.

Hub URL defaults to http://localhost:3001 and can be overridden via
window.__TELESCOPE_HUB_URL__.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Telescope
repository.description: Telescope: memory-efficient Kubernetes IDE (AKS-first) with desktop (Tauri) + web client

commit.message:
feat: add telescope-hub axum HTTP server (#102)

Headless HTTP/WebSocket server exposing Telescope engine capabilities
to web clients via REST API, replacing Tauri IPC.

Implements 15 endpoints:
- GET /healthz — health check
- GET /api/v1/contexts — list kubeconfig contexts
- POST /api/v1/connect — connect to a context
- POST /api/v1/disconnect — disconnect
- GET /api/v1/connection-state — current connection state
- GET /api/v1/resources — list resources by GVK
- GET /api/v1/pods — list pods
- GET /api/v1/events — list events
- GET /api/v1/namespaces — list namespaces
- GET /api/v1/pods/{ns}/{name}/logs — pod logs
- GET /api/v1/cluster-info — cluster version/auth info
- GET /api/v1/search — search cached resources
- GET /api/v1/helm/releases — list Helm releases
- GET /api/v1/metrics/pods — pod metrics
- GET /api/v1/crds — list CRDs
- GET /ws — WebSocket endpoint (echo/placeholder)

Includes Dockerfile for containerized deployment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Movement checks (#170)

* Replace wall collision penalty with proactive movement masking

- Add Room.can_link_move_from() for pixel-level movement checks using
  NES ObjGridOffset (tile checks only fire at grid boundaries)
- Add ZeldaGame.can_link_move() with locked-door-with-key override
- Add link_grid_offset to data.json (NES RAM 94)
- Remove WALL_COLLISION_PENALTY and _did_link_run_into_wall from critics
- Replace reactive _handle_wall_bump/move_mask in action_space with
  proactive per-direction can_link_move() calls in the mask builder
- Remove prevent_wall_bumping parameter from ZeldaActionSpace
- Replace stuck_count>=8 heuristic in frame_skip_wrapper with
  deterministic can_link_move_from() check at movement start
- Update tests: wall collision test → movement masking test,
  adjust stalfos injury timing for fewer wasted frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Category and Status management pages (#29)

Implements admin-only Category and Status management pages for Issue #23.

## Backend (Domain layer)
- MediatR Commands for Categories: CreateCategoryCommand, UpdateCategoryCommand, ArchiveCategoryCommand
- MediatR Queries for Categories: GetCategoriesQuery, GetCategoryByIdQuery
- MediatR Commands for Statuses: CreateStatusCommand, UpdateStatusCommand, ArchiveStatusCommand
- MediatR Queries for Statuses: GetStatusesQuery, GetStatusByIdQuery
- FluentValidation validators for create/update commands

## Services (Web layer)
- CategoryService: Facade for category operations via MediatR
- StatusService: Facade for status operations via MediatR

## Admin Pages (Blazor)
- /admin - Updated dashboard with navigation cards
- /admin/categories - Full CRUD for categories with archive/restore
- /admin/statuses - Full CRUD for statuses with archive/restore
- AdminPageLayout.razor - Shared admin navigation component

## Features
- Create, edit, archive/restore categories and statuses
- Show archived toggle filter
- Success/error message feedback
- Modal forms with validation
- Dark mode support
- Tailwind CSS styling consistent with existing pages
- Admin-only authorization via AdminPolicy

Closes #23

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(m65b): desktop finalization — expand watchers, container details, action gaps, tests

Expand Resource Watchers (was 3, now 13):
- Added real-time watching for Deployments, StatefulSets, DaemonSets,
  Services, ConfigMaps, Jobs, CronJobs, Ingresses, PVCs, ReplicaSets
- All resources now sync via live watch streams (not just on-demand)

Container Details:
- PodTable: Image column, enhanced status detection (CrashLoopBackOff,
  ImagePullBackOff, OOMKilled, init container states)
- Pod detail: resource requests/limits per container, owner references

Action Gaps Fixed:
- Rollout restart: now supports StatefulSets + DaemonSets (was Deployment-only)
- Rollout status: supports StatefulSets
- Delete: added Ingress, PVC, ReplicaSet support (was 9 types, now 12)

Integration Tests:
- Cluster-agnostic assertions (works on AKS and k3d)
- Added: can_list_deployments, can_list_services, metrics_api_check
- All 8 tests pass against live AKS cluster

99 Rust + 36 web = 135 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Task 1 — Rust unwrap() audit:

  helper that recovers from mutex poisoning via into_inner()
- helm.rs: Replace as_object().unwrap() with is_some_and() pattern;

fix: harden port-forward limits, exec output cap, and schema migrations

Port forward (S7 — #103):
- Add AtomicUsize counter, reject if >10 concurrent forwards
- Validate remote_port in range 1-65535
- Wrap accept loop with 1-hour idle timeout
- Decrement counter when forward task ends

Exec output (S8 — #104):
- Replace read_to_end with bounded_read (max 10MB per stream)
- Wrap entire exec in 5-minute timeout
- Return clear error messages for both limits

Schema migration (#105):
- Add schema_version table in init()
- Add get/set_schema_version helpers
- Add migrate() runner called on open() after init()
- Version 1 = current schema; future migrations slot in sequentially

Closes #103, closes #104, closes #105

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix security vulnerabilities in auth endpoints (closes #13) (#15)

Security Fixes:
- Add open redirect prevention: Validate returnUrl is local path before redirecting
- Change logout from GET to POST: Prevents CSRF logout attacks
- Update LoginDisplay.razor: Use form with POST method and AntiforgeryToken

Implementation Details:
- Added IsLocalUrl() helper to validate URLs start with / but not //
- Rejects http://, https://, and protocol-relative URLs
- Logout endpoint now requires POST method
- Antiforgery validation via UseAntiforgery() middleware

Testing:
- Added Web.Tests project with integration tests
- Tests verify URL validation logic for open redirect protection
- Tests verify GET logout returns 404 (endpoint removed)
- Tests verify POST logout requires authentication
- All 12 tests passing

References:
- OWASP Open Redirect: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Unvalidated_Redirects_and_Forwards_Cheat_Sheet.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update cli/db_restore.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update git submodules

* Update manila from branch 'master'
  to e6a04c9b64f976520791dddb628f5e968b83c8a8
  - Merge "Fix generic driver NFS share deletion 'target is busy' error"
  - Fix generic driver NFS share deletion 'target is busy' error
    
    When deleting NFS shares from generic driver service VMs, the
    operation can fail with 'target is busy' because NFS exports are
    not unexported before unmount is attempted.
    
    This patch fixes the issue by:
    
    1. Implementing NFSHelper.remove_exports() to actively unexport all
       NFS clients via 'exportfs -u' before the share is unmounted.
       Previously this method was a no-op.
    
    2. Adding a lazy unmount fallback in GenericShareDriver._unmount_device()
       that detects 'is busy' errors and retries with 'umount -l' to allow
       the unmount to succeed even when NFS kernel state hasn't fully
       drained.
    
    Assisted-By: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
    Change-Id: I9e31a0e082c50ab24b3f01522aba7bb1fb2eaa64
    Signed-off-by: Yaguang Tang <heut2008@gmail.com>

[Repo Assist] fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786) (#1492)

* fix: resolve type-forwarded types when decompiling (closes #786)

When go-to-definition is used on a type that lives in a different
assembly from the one FCS reports (e.g. System.Boolean forwarded from
netstandard.dll to System.Runtime.dll), ILSpy's CSharpDecompiler
throws:
  'Decompiling types that are not part of the main module is not supported'

This happens because ILSpy's type system resolves type forwarders
transparently — GetTypeDefinition returns the actual type definition
from the target assembly's module, but the decompiler was created for
the source assembly. The mismatch causes the exception.

Fix: after resolving the type definition, check whether its
ParentModule matches the decompiler's main module. If not (a type
forwarder), retrieve the actual assembly path from
IModule.PEFile.FileName and create a fresh CSharpDecompiler for
that assembly. Resolve the type definition again in the new type
system, then decompile as normal.

If the PEFile path is unavailable or the type cannot be resolved in
the redirected assembly, the original decompiler and type definition
are used unchanged — the existing try/catch will handle any remaining
failure gracefully.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

---------

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] fix: skip unreadable files in rename; remove duplicate log in textDocument/implementation (#1491)

* fix: skip unreadable files in rename and log warnings in textDocument/implementation

Fixes two code quality issues in AdaptiveFSharpLspServer.fs:

1. TextDocumentRename: skip files that cannot be read
   When building workspace edits for rename, if a file cannot be opened
   (e.g., SourceLinked virtual paths like /_/src/Server.fs from external
   packages that FCS references but don't exist locally), return None and
   filter it out rather than generating edits for an unreadable file.
   This prevents client errors when the LSP client tries to apply edits
   to files that don't exist. See issue #1011.

2. TextDocumentImplementation: remove duplicate log + log warning on failure
   - Remove the duplicate 'TextDocumentImplementation Request: {params}'
     log that was emitted twice in the handler.
   - Log a warning when getProjectOptions fails instead of silently calling
     failwith, making these failures visible in server logs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
Add comprehensive WithExternalAccess() tests for #102

- Idempotency: calling WithExternalAccess() twice causes no errors, endpoints stay external
- Order independence: document that WithExternalAccess() before WithBlueMap() does NOT mark BlueMap external (by design  iterates current annotations)
- Fluent chain: both .WithBlueMap().WithExternalAccess() and .WithExternalAccess().WithBlueMap() compile and work correctly
- Unrelated endpoints: custom endpoints are not affected by WithExternalAccess()
- All three endpoints: combined assertion when BlueMap + ExternalAccess called in correct order
- Total: 13 WithExternalAccess tests, all passing

to mention this alongside cluster list.
